diagnosis: Agent optimizes for fast descent (progress reward) over landing quality, resulting in high-speed one-leg touchdowns (10% falls, 41% both_legs_down) because speed penalty is negligible (-0.066) and there is no penalty for angular instability

proposed_changes.current_code: r_speed = -speed * 0.05  # Penalize high velocity to encourage throttle modulation
proposed_changes.new_code: r_speed = -speed * 0.3  # Penalize high velocity to encourage controlled descent
proposed_changes.reason: Current speed penalty (-0.066 at speed 1.32) is 18x smaller than progress reward (~1.228), providing insufficient counter-pressure. Increasing to 0.3 makes speed penalty (-0.396) comparable to other components, incentivizing the agent to slow down for stable landings.

proposed_changes.current_code: No angle penalty exists in the reward function
proposed_changes.new_code:
r_angle = -abs(state[4]) * 0.5 - abs(state[5]) * 0.3  # Penalize tilt and angular velocity for stable landing posture
components["r_angle"] = r_angle
proposed_changes.reason: Agent lands at arbitrary angles (mean angle 0.047 rad, angular velocity 0.088) without penalty. Adding angle penalty creates incentive to maintain upright posture during descent, increasing both_legs_down rate and reducing falls at touchdown.

predicted_effect: Agent will descend more slowly and maintain upright posture, increasing both_legs_down rate from ~41% to ~70% and reducing fall rate from 10% to <5%, while maintaining 90%+ completion rate
max_risk: If penalties are too strong, agent may hover indefinitely to avoid speed/angle penalties, reducing completion rate and increasing episode length
risk_mitigation: Monitor mean_length and completion_rate: if mean_length exceeds 600 or completion drops below 80%, reduce r_speed coefficient from 0.3 to 0.2 and r_angle coefficients from 0.5/0.3 to 0.3/0.2
